title: PCIe Host-Device Transfer Bandwidth

question.ja: PCIe 5.0 x16 の理論片方向帯域に対して H2D と D2H の実効帯域はどれくらい出るか。また pageable host memory と pinned host memory で何倍差がつくか。
question.en: How much effective H2D and D2H bandwidth is achieved relative to the theoretical one-way bandwidth of PCIe 5.0 x16, and how large is the pageable-vs-pinned host memory gap?

focus: PCIe 5.0 x16 theoretical one-way bandwidth utilization
Pinned vs pageable host-memory transfer gap
Difference between H2D and D2H transfer behavior

comparisons.ja: H2D と D2H の比較
pinned と pageable の比較
実測帯域と PCIe 5.0 x16 理論帯域の比較
comparisons.en: Measured bandwidth vs PCIe 5.0 x16 theoretical limit

considerations.ja: Pinned と pageable の差が 2〜3 倍以上出る主因として、pageable 側では CUDA ドライバが内部で一時的な page-locked staging buffer を介してコピーを行い、余分なメモリコピーと CPU 側オーバーヘッドが加わる可能性が高い。一方で pinned host memory は GPU DMA 転送にそのまま使いやすく、PCIe リンク帯域に近づきやすい。
considerations.en: A major reason the pinned-vs-pageable gap can reach 2-3x or more is that pageable transfers often require an internal page-locked staging buffer inside the CUDA driver, adding an extra host-side copy and CPU overhead. Pinned host memory is easier to use directly for GPU DMA transfers and therefore tends to get closer to the PCIe link limit.

considerations.ja: 理論値との比較では、PCIe 5.0 x16 の片方向理論値 63.015 GB/s は約 58.69 GiB/s に相当する。今回の pinned 実測は H2D 約 52.42 GiB/s、D2H 約 52.13 GiB/s なので、理論値比ではおよそ 89% 前後まで出ており、乖離はあるが『かなり大きい』というほどではない。リンク層 overhead、プロトコル overhead、実装上の効率低下を考えると妥当な水準とみなせる。
considerations.en: For the theoretical comparison, the one-way PCIe 5.0 x16 limit of 63.015 GB/s corresponds to about 58.69 GiB/s. In this run, pinned transfers reached about 52.42 GiB/s for H2D and 52.13 GiB/s for D2H, which is roughly 89% of the theoretical limit. There is still a gap, but it is not unusually large; it is consistent with link-layer overhead, protocol overhead, and normal implementation inefficiency.

considerations.ja: 一方で pageable 実測は H2D 約 19.41 GiB/s、D2H 約 15.39 GiB/s と理論値比で 26〜33% 程度にとどまる。したがって今回の主なボトルネックは PCIe 自体ではなく、pageable メモリ経由の転送パスにあると解釈するのが自然である。
considerations.en: By contrast, pageable transfers reached only about 19.41 GiB/s for H2D and 15.39 GiB/s for D2H, which is only around 26-33% of the theoretical limit. That suggests the main bottleneck in this run is not the PCIe link itself, but the pageable-memory transfer path.

considerations.ja: 今回の最大 speedup は約 4.54x だが、サイズごとの値を見ると小サイズでは初期 overhead の影響が大きく、サイズが大きくなると pinned は 50 GiB/s 超で plateau しやすい。したがって代表値を見るときは単一サイズではなく、大サイズでの plateau と speedup の安定域を重視するべきである。
considerations.en: The maximum speedup in this run is about 4.54x, but the per-size values show that small transfers are more affected by fixed overhead. As transfer size grows, pinned transfers settle into a plateau above 50 GiB/s. For interpretation, it is better to focus on the large-size plateau and the stable speedup region rather than on a single size point.
